title: Planogram assisted inventory system and method
abstract: A manually assisted robot inventory monitoring method provides for detecting and reading shelf labels using an autonomous robot. Bounding boxes around possible products in a panoramic image can be taken with at least one camera associated with the autonomous robot. Products in the bounding boxes are automatically identified, with those that are not being later manually identified.
claims:
Claims IN THE CLAIMS 1 . A planogram assisted inventory method, comprising the steps of: providing an initial planogram with identified products; creating a panoramic image including products on a shelf, with possible products being surrounded by bounding boxes; detecting and reading shelf labels to localize and associate possible products with both the initial planogram and the possible products in the bounding boxes; and identifying the possible products with a product classifier. 2. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 , further comprising the step of segmenting images to extract product images and identifiers and associate with the initial planogram. 3. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 , further comprising the step of template matching possible products in the bounding boxes without product segmentation. 4. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 , further comprising the step of using vision based product recognition to extract features descriptors for comparison with possible products in the bounding boxes. 5. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 , further comprising the step of using deep learning method to build classifiers that identify products or gaps. 6. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 , further comprising the step of allowing an autonomous robot to move beside the shelf, with the autonomous robot acting as a movable base capable of both autonomously detecting and reading shelf labels and creating the panoramic image. 7. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 , further comprising the step of allowing an autonomous robot to move beside a shelf, with the autonomous robot acting as a movable base to capture a depth map of shelves and of products positioned on shelves using 3D cameras, a LIDAR system or structure from motion and multiple cameras. 8. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 , further comprising the step of using manual input to identify possible products designated in the bounding boxes. 9. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 , wherein the product classifier can include at least one of a machine learning system or a deep learning system. 10. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 , further comprising the step of identifying stock depletion level for a product using a product depth detection system. 1 1. A manually assisted robot inventory monitoring method, comprising the steps of: detecting and reading shelf labels using an autonomous robot; defining bounding boxes around possible products in a panoramic image taken with at least one camera associated with the autonomous robot; automatically identifying products in the bounding boxes; and 

 manually identifying products in the bounding boxes that were not automatically identified. 12. The manually assisted robot inventory monitoring method of claim 1 1 , further comprising the step of using manual input to identify potential inventory designated in the multiple bounding boxes in the image taken with at least one camera 13. The manually assisted robot inventory monitoring method of claim 1 1 , wherein the bounding boxes can surround gaps between possible products. 14. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 1 , wherein the step of automatically identifying products can further comprise use of at least one of a machine learning or a deep learning system. 15. The planogram assisted inventory method of claim 1 1 , further comprising the step of identifying stock depletion level for an automatically or manually identified product using a product depth detection system. 16. An inventory system comprising: a planogram with identified products; an autonomous robot able to capture a panoramic image including products on a shelf, with possible products being identified by bounding boxes; a shelf label detector and reader attached to the autonomous robot and able to localize and associate possible products with both the planogram and the possible products in the bounding boxes; and a product classifier to use information from the planogram and the possible products in the bounding boxes to identify products in the panoramic image. 17. The inventory system of claim 16, further comprising an image segmentation system able to extract product images and identifiers and associate them with the planogram. 18. The inventory system of claim 16, further comprising a template matching system to match possible products in the bounding boxes without product segmentation. 19. The inventory system of claim 16, further comprising a system for vision based product recognition to extract features descriptors for comparison with possible products in the bounding boxes. 20. The inventory system of claim 16, further comprising a deep learning system to build classifiers that identify products.
